     The Numbers Business Report

Star Trek Into Darkness (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $228,778,661Details
International Box Office $238,602,923
Worldwide Box Office $467,381,584
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $28,479,666
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $52,461,620
Total $80,941,286
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In the wake of a shocking act of terror from within their own organization, the crew of The Enterprise is called back home to Earth. In defiance of regulations and with a personal score to settle, Captain Kirk leads his crew on a manhunt to capture an unstoppable force of destruction and bring those responsible to justice. As our heroes are propelled into an epic chess game of life and death, love will be challenged, friendships will be torn apart, and sacrifices must be made for the only family Kirk has left: his crew

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$70,165,559 (30.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.26 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:48.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$190,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,868 opening theaters/3,907 max. theaters, 5.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $336,555,082

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 15th, 2013 (Sneak Peek) by Paramount Pictures
May 16th, 2013 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
May 16th, 2013 (IMAX) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: November 12th, 2020 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: September 10th, 2013 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of sci - fi action and violence.
(Rating bulletin 2269, 4/24/2013)
Running Time: 132 minutes
Franchise: Star Trek
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Intertitle, Invented Language, Prologue
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Filming location (country): United States
Geographical setting: San Francisco
Plot point: Genetic Engineering, Government Corruption, Humans as Aliens, Secret Agent, Secret Identity, Superhuman, Surprise Twist, Terrorism
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on TV
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Bad Robot, Skydance Productions, Paramount Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Klingon

Synopsis

In the wake of a shocking act of terror from within their own organization, the crew of The Enterprise is called back home to Earth. In defiance of regulations and with a personal score to settle, Captain Kirk leads his crew on a manhunt to capture an unstoppable force of destruction and bring those responsible to justice. As our heroes are propelled into an epic chess game of life and death, love will be challenged, friendships will be torn apart, and sacrifices must be made for the only family Kirk has left: his crew
